title: Surfboard Coffee Table
summary: A low 1x3-plank table with double-beveled splayed legs and rounded, jigsawed corners that read as a surfboard — sized to work as a coffee table, bench, or kids' table.
description:
The legs are the one tricky part: each is cut with a genuine double bevel — 5° off square and a 5° bevel at the same time — so mark the inside corner of every leg the moment it's cut, since it's remarkably easy to lose track of which way a leg faces once you've cut all four with such a subtle angle. **You can build this without the double bevel**, but the plan is direct that the beveled version is sturdier and holds up better, especially under kids treating it as a pretend surfboard.

Predrill and attach the aprons with glue and 2-1/2" screws, adding corner bracing if the assembled frame has any wobble. Once the tabletop boards are nailed down, round the corners and cut the surfboard's characteristic shape with a jigsaw, then sand the edges smooth.

**The surfboard graphic on top is an actual pattern, not a suggestion** — the source provides a printable graph you transfer square by square onto the tabletop, then paint in a contrasting color, so the finished look is closer to a real board than a generic plank table.

tools.note: For rounding the corners into the surfboard shape.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain, plus contrasting paint for the graphic

cutList.note: Both ends cut 5° off square with a 5° bevel, ends parallel to each other.
Both ends cut at 5° off square, not parallel, short point to short point.
Both ends cut at 5° off square, not parallel, short point to short point.

steps.title: Cut and mark the legs
steps.body: Cut the four legs with the double bevel, and mark the inside corner of each leg as soon as it's cut to keep track of orientation.

steps.title: Attach the aprons
steps.body: Predrill and attach the short and long aprons to the legs with glue and 2-1/2" screws. Add corner bracing if there's any wobble.

steps.title: Attach the center supports
steps.body: Attach the center supports between the aprons.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop and shape it
steps.body: Nail down the tabletop boards, round the corners, and cut the surfboard shape with a jigsaw. Sand the edges smooth.

steps.title: Finish and add the graphic
steps.body: Sand, stain, or paint the piece. Transfer the printable surfboard graphic onto the tabletop square by square and paint in a contrasting color.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain, plus contrasting paint for the graphic

images.alt: Surfboard-shaped coffee table with a painted graphic
